purpose: Garment registry for Tunya, including marital and life-stage signaling. Apparel is a code: how someone is dressed tells you their species, marital status, life stage, profession, and increasingly their political affiliation.

garments.description: Blubber encased in leather; soft-leather-covered jute twine securing through toes or across foot; secured at ankles.
Sturdier than shoes — wood under blubber/leather, wood held by short nails.
Worn by unioned women not currently breastfeeding.
Worn by unioned women — fabric around neck and breasts, knotted at side or behind neck.
Tight-fit body, short sleeves, low neck, cropped to navel — easy to nurse.
Worn by children and post-childbearing women.
Two kinds — cold-weather shawl + wind-protection shawl.
Shirt for males; sleeveless or short/long sleeves.
Bosom to ankle, slits at sides for walking.
Tight bosom, billowing skirt to ankle.
Long embroidered pleated skirt; bare lower back/midriff; light materials.
Hip-to-ankle loose skirt, bold-stripe colored fabrics, linen/cotton.
Wraps around skirts/dresses, sheer muslin or silk.
Worn by children under 2 annums; onesie-like; cotton/light wool.
Worn by little girls 3-15 annums; similar to bosom dress.
Wrapped at waist, thrown over shoulder. The way it is worn signals marital status. Doubles as sun shield.
garments.marital_signal: EXPLICIT — the wrap pattern itself encodes union status, fidelity, widowhood, and openness to courtship
garments.cultural_significance: the most-readable diplomatic garment on Tunya. A foreign delegate who cannot read sari-wraps will misread an Akeia treaty chamberlain entirely.

unioned_nursing: choli with sari thrown over single shoulder
post_childbearing: sari worn high-waist with covered shoulder
widowed: sari worn full-shoulder, color: brown (mourning) for 1 annum, then any color
permanently_unbonded_by_choice: camisole + tunic-style overshirt; the kingdom's signal varies

life_stage_garments.elder: kingdom-specific honorifics layered on the standard adult set

kingdom_silhouette_priors.comment: Each kingdom favors specific garment subsets based on its founding population's cultural origin. Cross-kingdom blends are normal in border regions.
medici_isle: robed garment (covers Medici male skull cone in public)
long flowing sari (Medici female long hair displayed via sari over shoulder)
asbir: neoclassical formal wear for civic functions
dormas: couture-tailored garments — every garment above, but in fitted, dyed, embroidered versions; servant-nobility wear specific livery; the actual upper class wear something else, see lore
nil: woven-bark robe — the only kingdom whose primary garment is unique to it; pure Tunyans wear no other
akeia_of_kahlay: ceremonial sari + lehenga (queen mother and consorts)
